description:
Letter in the hand of Avraham Maimonides (d. 1237) or his son David asking a cantor to arrange a collection in the synagogue on a Thursday morning for two chickens and bread for a poor, old, sick man. (Information from Mediterranean Society, II, p. 463, and from Amir Ashur; cf. T-S Misc.8.18, written in a similar hand and layout.)

Dating: 13th century
tags: Avraham Maimonides, Illness, medical charity, communal, David I Maimonides, chicken, illness letter 969-1517, Appeal
